name: Dinosaurs
description: Ancient giants — species, paleontology, and dino pop culture

questions.q: The word 'dinosaur' comes from Greek roots meaning what?

source.notes: From Greek 'deinos' (terrible) and 'sauros' (lizard).

questions.q: How many horns did a Triceratops have on its head?

source.notes: A short nose horn and two longer brow horns.

questions.q: How did dinosaurs reproduce?

source.notes: Dinosaurs were egg-layers; well-preserved fossilized eggs have been found on every continent.

questions.q: In which year did the original 'Jurassic Park' film hit theaters?

source.notes: Directed by Steven Spielberg; adapted from Michael Crichton's 1990 novel.

questions.q: What is the name for a scientist who studies dinosaur fossils and other ancient life?

source.notes: Paleontology = the study of ancient life through fossils.

questions.q: Dinosaurs belong to which broad class of animals?

source.notes: Modern classification places birds within the reptile lineage as living dinosaurs.

questions.q: The non-avian dinosaurs went extinct approximately how many million years ago?

source.notes: The K-Pg extinction event ~66 million years ago; often rounded to 65 million.

questions.q: Which British anatomist coined the term 'dinosaur' in 1842?

source.notes: Owen founded the Natural History Museum in London.

questions.q: 'Spinosaurus' literally translates to what?

source.notes: Named for the large neural spines forming a sail on its back.

questions.q: 'Stegosaurus' translates roughly to what?

source.notes: Early paleontologists thought the back plates lay flat like roof tiles.

questions.q: Apatosaurus was for decades better known by which now-partially-rehabilitated name?

source.notes: Brontosaurus was classed as a synonym for Apatosaurus in 1903; a 2015 study argued for re-separating them.

questions.q: Throughout the Triassic Period, Earth's landmass was consolidated into a single supercontinent known as what?

source.notes: Began breaking up in the late Triassic, roughly 200 million years ago.

questions.q: The first Velociraptor fossil was discovered in 1923 in which country?

source.notes: Found during an American Museum of Natural History expedition in the Gobi Desert.

questions.q: Which actor played Dr. Alan Grant in the original 'Jurassic Park' and its 2022 return, 'Jurassic World: Dominion'?

source.notes: New Zealand actor; Grant is a paleontologist.

questions.q: In the 'Jurassic World' film trilogy, Chris Pratt's character Owen Grady has a special bond with which dinosaurs?

source.notes: He trains a pack of four including the lead raptor Blue.

questions.q: Based on fossil evidence, what kind of diet did most dinosaurs have?

source.notes: Most known dinosaur species were plant-eaters.

questions.q: What is the full Latin binomial for the iconic 'T. Rex'?

source.notes: Name means 'Tyrant Lizard King'.

questions.q: Triceratops's name translates literally to what?

source.notes: From Greek 'tri' (three), 'keras' (horn), and 'ops' (face).

questions.q: Which state has officially designated Stegosaurus as its state dinosaur?

source.notes: Designated in 1982; many Stegosaurus fossils have been unearthed at Morrison Formation sites.

questions.q: In the Super Mario video game series, what kind of creature is Mario's sidekick Yoshi?

source.notes: Debuted in Super Mario World (1990).

questions.q: In which U.S. state was the most complete Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton, nicknamed 'Sue', discovered in 1990?

source.notes: Found by Sue Hendrickson; now displayed at the Field Museum in Chicago.

questions.q: The famous Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton 'Sue' is on permanent display at which museum?
choices: American Museum of Natural History, NYC

source.notes: The Field Museum purchased the fossil at auction in 1997 for about $8.3 million.
